card_description: Empedocles (Greek: "Glory of the people") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Empedocles is pre-socratic philosopher of love and strife. Referenced in PKD's discussions of cosmic forces in tension. Appears in 6 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Empedocles. Context: "Dick identifies the "Second Advent" as a "Secret Kingdom" existing in eternity while the "usurper" (Satan/Empire) remains in control of linear time."
